title: Maximum Frequency of an Element After Performing Operations I
content:
You are given an integer array nums and two integers k and numOperations.

You must perform an operation numOperations times on nums, where in each operation you:

• Select an index i that was not selected in any previous operations.

• Add an integer in the range [-k, k] to nums[i].

Return the maximum possible frequency of any element in nums after performing the operations.

Example 1:

Input: nums = [1,4,5], k = 1, numOperations = 2

Output: 2

Explanation: We can achieve a maximum frequency of two by:

• Adding 0 to nums[1]. nums becomes [1, 4, 5].

• Adding -1 to nums[2]. nums becomes [1, 4, 4].

Example 2:

Input: nums = [5,11,20,20], k = 5, numOperations = 1

Output: 2

Explanation: We can achieve a maximum frequency of two by:

• Adding 0 to nums[1].

Constraints:

• 1 <= nums.length <= 105

• 1 <= nums[i] <= 105

• 0 <= k <= 105

• 0 <= numOperations <= nums.length

codeSnippets.code:
class Solution(object):
    def maxFrequency(self, nums, k, numOperations):
        """
        :type nums: List[int]
        :type k: int
        :type numOperations: int
        :rtype: int
        """

hints: Sort the array and try each value in range as a candidate.

solutions.python:
import bisect

class Solution(object):
    def maxFrequency(self, nums, k, numOperations):
        nums.sort()
        n = len(nums)
        # For a target value t, count elements that can reach t:
        # elements in [t-k, t+k]. Among these, existing elements equal to t
        # don't need an operation; others need one.
        # Max frequency = (count of nums[i]==t) + min(numOperations, count in [t-k,t+k] - count of t)
        # Candidate targets: each unique value in nums, and each nums[i]+k, nums[i]-k.
        from collections import Counter
        freq = Counter(nums)
        candidates = set()
        for v in nums:
            candidates.add(v)
            candidates.add(v + k)
            candidates.add(v - k)

        ans = 0
        for t in candidates:
            # Count elements in [t-k, t+k]
            lo = bisect.bisect_left(nums, t - k)
            hi = bisect.bisect_right(nums, t + k)
            in_range = hi - lo
            exact = freq.get(t, 0)
            need_ops = in_range - exact
            ans = max(ans, exact + min(numOperations, need_ops))
        return ans
solutions.explanation:
For each candidate target t, count elements already equal to t (free) and elements in [t-k, t+k] that need an operation to become t. Use binary search on the sorted array. Candidate targets are existing values ± k (optimal targets are always at these boundaries).

Time: O(n log n). Space: O(n).
